claims.claim: Zero-knowledge proofs / SNARKs are compounding fast; cheaper proving is the unlock
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Layer-2 scaling / rollups as the prerequisite for cheap block space
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Wallet UX, smart accounts & self-sovereign identity will reset the crypto frontend
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Crypto × AI / content provenance (proving origin/history of content needs crypto)
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Decentralized social as a neutral, un-ruggable platform
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Concern: opaque centralized exchanges & custodial systems / proof-of-reserves theater
claims.label: absent

claims.claim: Concern: token-first projects / 'tokenomics' — token-without-substance is a failure pattern
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: ETH — conviction in the Ethereum network and its scaling roadmap
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Risk-on: skeptical in analysis, optimistic in action; 'still early, so I build'
claims.label: confirmed

missed_changes: Newly vocal enthusiasm for crypto PAYMENTS / stablecoins (cheap USDC transfers, USDC as gas), triggered by Apple/EU app-store rulings — 'Payments has been one of the goals of crypto from the beginning. The pieces are finally coming together.' This sector was not in the prediction.
New advocacy for crypto SECURITY institutions/tooling: co-founding the Security Alliance and the Whitehat Safe Harbor Agreement, plus consumer account-security (2FA) guidance — not anticipated.
Newly visible interest in DeFi / AMM mechanism design (LVR, DELV's fixed/variable-rate AMM, Morpho) — an area the prediction did not cover.

Strong, specific embrace of proof-of-personhood (Worldcoin) as necessary infrastructure for the AI-content/sybil problem — a sharper, named bet than the prediction's general 'self-sovereign identity' / 'content provenance' framing.
notes: Core theses all held and were heavily acted on, not just restated: ZK (Jolt Alpha zkVM, Naysayer proofs, Binius), L2 scaling (Helios L2 extension, Optimism superchain podcast), Ethereum conviction (Helios light client), crypto×AI provenance ('mystery meat' scenario), and risk-on build-mode ('never been a better time to build'). No contradictions or reversals. The token-first concern is marked confirmed because his sustained, forceful anti-memecoin campaign ('not technically interesting,' 'fake tech to mask token liquidity schemes,' deters founders / arms regulators) is the same underlying stance — token-without-real-protocol-substance is hollow/damaging — even though it manifested as memecoin criticism rather than the predicted 'tokenomics consulting'; the intensity/specific arguments were a notable new manifestation. On-chain games is confirmed but thin (only an EVE Project Awakening playtest share). Decentralized social confirmed via endorsement of River protocol/Propeller ('user-owned chat communities, I dig it') rather than the predicted Farcaster. The centralized-exchange/custodial concern was not restated in the window (window's centralization comments are about content censorship and corporate platform capture, a different domain), so it is absent — consistent with its 'persisted, not restated' provenance.
